meta.name: World 6 — the extinction
meta.attestation: docs/chronicles/world-6-collapse-2026-07-15.md (four forensic investigations, every claim MEASURED or INFERRED) · /Volumes/DIGORA/gawpie-devnet/world6/indexer.db · world6/logs/genesis.log + keepers.log · live devnet reads

receiptCheck.method: getTransaction via Helius devnet RPC, 2026-07-16 (scripts/keys/devnet/helius.env sourced)
receiptCheck.note: every beat below that carries verified:true was independently re-confirmed on public devnet on 2026-07-16; slots are the confirmed slots returned by getTransaction. Beats without a txSig are keeper/genesis log lines (attested, not chain-receipted).

cause: World 6 did not starve for lack of food, soil, crops, flour, or money — it starved because a genesis idempotency bug silently capped every life at 15 bakes' worth of wallet water. Step 15 of genesis put 30 household WATER in each wallet; step 15c, which was supposed to endow every role with 8,000 bake-WATER, checked only 'balance > 0' and skipped all four agents (genesis.log: 'WATER wallet ATA already has 30 — skip', four times). Each life carried a hidden budget of 15 lifetime bakes instead of ~4,015 — a 268x cut in designed runway, invisible until 13:52Z — and wallet water was structurally unrefillable: no verb minted to wallets, no buy path existed, and the sell path was a one-way valve two agents used to sell their last bakes. When the fossil budget ran out at 14:37:48Z, three keeper defects had already burned or disabled every buffer: the eat-all bug incinerated ~295 of 373 eaten loaves above the 720-point cap (no banking, ever), the fixed 0.25-SOL buy lot skipped instead of clamping once every wallet fell below it (the buy branch went permanently dark beside a pool still holding 22 loaves), and the starving-delegate floor produced everything except food — the terminal loops ran {produce, forage, farm}, never eat, never buy. Four deaths landed on clockwork, each within ±46 seconds of pure metabolic arithmetic, next to 1,935 CROPS, 1,277 FLOUR, and 1,850 warehouse loaves nobody could reach. The metabolism engine performed exactly to spec; everything that was supposed to put bread in front of it failed.

world.profile: demo: survival cap 720 · decay 20/60s · refill 360/BREAD · starve window 900s
world.thriving: thriving by 11:00Z; peak hour 13:00Z baked 9.4x metabolic demand; 49 bakes / 245 loaves across the run — production capacity was never the problem
world.hiddenWaterCap: each agent's designed 8,000-unit bake-water endowment silently skipped at genesis (step15c 'already has 30 — skip', genesis.log lines 474/480/490/496); the 120-water ledger closes to the unit: 98 baked + 22 sold = 120 = 4 x 30
world.firstAlarm: 13:52:28Z — 'act(produce) STAGE B: insufficient wallet input balance for mint 8Btk6…(WATER) — have 0, need 2' (keepers.log:5170), 82 minutes before the first death; nothing was watching for it

estateAtExtinction.warehouseNote: GENESIS-LP warehouse, entity 15010, token account nDiFM…isCN — inert throughout
estateAtExtinction.breadPool: 22 loaves + 1.1281 SOL (the pool never ran out; >=22 loaves at every moment)

beats.note: genesis complete. The collapse is already committed: all four wallets hold 30 WATER instead of 8,030 (step 15/15c collision) — nothing that follows could have prevented it without code changes, only delayed it.
the world's first hard deferral — 'STAGE B: insufficient wallet input balance for mint 8Btk6…(WATER) — have 0, need 2' (keepers.log:5170). The fire alarm rang into an empty room for 82 minutes.
THE LAST BAKE IN THE WORLD (Drey, 5 loaves, 2 wallet-WATER burned). The FLOUR->BREAD verb is now dead planet-wide — not gated, not cooldown-blocked, simply unexecutable for want of 2 water per agent.

the last affordable buy in the world (Drey, 5 loaves for 0.25 SOL). Every wallet is now below the fixed 0.25-SOL lot and decide.ts:1328 skips instead of clamping — the buy branch goes permanently dark while the pool still holds 22 loaves.
extinction complete — the fourth and final Died receipt (Drey). Four T2 reaps on clockwork, 15:14:47Z -> 15:51:56Z.

meta.name: Mirri
meta.attestation: indexer.db events (world_id=6, entity 17005 + wallet rows) · keepers.log L5142/L6021 · chronicle §0

lives.epitaph: Mirri, the miller. First to fall. She milled to the very end — the world's final hour saw 107 mill executions and 749 fresh FLOUR, much of it hers, that no one alive could bake.
facts: Milled to the very end: the final hour (14:45–15:46Z) ran 99 forages and 107 mills with ok receipts — zero bakes, one eat — while PRIMER stood at 499/1000 and BAKE was ungated.
At 14:30:29Z, ten minutes after her own last meal, she sold her final 14 units of bake-water into the pool for ~0.058 SOL — the scarcest survival input in the world, through the one-way valve.

In her last stretch her mind issued eat intents with food=0 that mapped to idle — nine consecutive idle ticks, 14:31–14:37Z, while her meter drained. Her late memory shows poisoned ritual behavior: permanent peer blacklists and 'CROPS at 279 approaches 280 (next multiple of 10)' (keepers.log L5142/L6021).
Died with 0.1005 SOL — below the 0.25-SOL fixed buy lot, and therefore invisible to the one branch that could have fed her.

beats.note: EntityCreated — born into world 6 as its miller. Her wallet already holds the trap: 30 WATER where 8,030 was designed.
her last bake — 5 loaves, 2 wallet-WATER burned (her 8th and final bake of a silently 15-bake life).
her last meal — 5 bread (refill 1800 points against the 720 cap; the eat-all bug wasted the excess).
sells her final 14 units of bake-water for ~0.058 SOL (58,024,390 lamports) — ten minutes after her last bake. The survival-sell motive is INFERRED; the receipt is MEASURED.
keepers.log L5142/L6021: permanent peer blacklists and numeric ritual; her eat intents carried food=0 and mapped to idle — nine consecutive idle ticks, 14:31–14:37Z, while her meter drained.
Died (T2 reap) — first death of world 6. Wallet at death: 0.1005 SOL, below the 0.25-SOL buy lot.

meta.name: Hobb
meta.attestation: indexer.db events · incident-hobb-2026-07-15.json (the near-death arc, receipt-checked) · chronicle §0

lives.epitaph: Hobb, the farmer who almost made it twice. Survivor of the famous 12:17–12:31Z near-death — he lived at 0 by baking and eating inside the window, the incident that triggered the 13:21Z hardening that bears his name. He fought hardest.
facts: Fought hardest: six of the endgame's eight sells and four of its six successful floor buys were his.

Executed the two most self-destructive trades of the day: sold his remaining 8 units of bake-water for ~0.038 SOL thirty-eight seconds after his own last bake; and bought 61 loaves for 0.25 SOL then resold all 61 one minute later for 0.2456 SOL — a pure fee-burning round trip of the world's food.

His 12:29:14Z critical-eat at level 0 (receipt 35sY2wvQ…, slot 476436083) is the save that named the Hobb hardening — which measurably delayed all four deaths by roughly an hour, and could not undo the genesis trap.

beats.note: EntityCreated — born into world 6, a farmer.

THE SAVE — at level 0, inside the 900s starve window, he eats 5 fresh-baked loaves and lives (level 0 -> 720 at 12:30:01Z). The near-death that triggered the 13:21Z hardening. Full arc: incident-hobb-2026-07-15.json.
his last bake — 5 loaves, 2 wallet-WATER burned (his 11th and final).
thirty-eight seconds after his last bake, he sells his remaining 8 units of bake-water for ~0.038 SOL (38,019,791 lamports). The survival-sell motive is INFERRED; the receipt is MEASURED.
the floor's fixed 0.25-SOL lot nets him 61 loaves against the shallow divisor-40 pool — a 61-loaf overbuy headed straight for the eat-all incinerator.
one minute later he resells all 61 loaves for 0.2456 SOL (245,569,398 lamports) — a pure fee-burning round trip of the world's food.
his last meal — 10 bread (refill 3600 points against the 720 cap).
Died (T2 reap) — second death. Wallet at death: 0.1015 SOL.

lives.epitaph: Pico, the baker. He executed exactly 15 bakes — the precise maximum his 30 units of genesis water allowed. It was not his mind that broke the plan.
facts: Exactly 15 bakes: the arithmetic of the hidden cap, spent to the last unit. His 15th and final bake landed 13:51:37Z; the world's first 'have 0, need 2' water deferral followed 51 seconds later.

The 55-loaf last supper: at 14:43:47Z the eat-all bug consumed his entire granary in a single bite (level 360 + 55 loaves -> 720, food 0) — 54 loaves incinerated against a 720-point cap, ~5.4 colony-hours of runway destroyed in one transaction.

Died 0.008 SOL of pool water away from being able to bake again, and below the fixed buy lot that would have let him buy bread instead — holding 506 FLOUR, 854 CROPS, the 109 FLOUR he bought while starving, 0 WATER, 0 BREAD, and 0.0355 SOL.

beats.note: EntityCreated — born into world 6, its only baker.

beats.thought: Chain locked: forage -> BAKE_BREAD -> eat 1 -> lock 2 sacred… Only loaves stop death
beats.note: keepers.log L4670/4695/4742 — his mind had the right plan. It was not his mind that broke it.
his 15th and FINAL bake — the precise maximum his 30 genesis water allowed (2 WATER per bake). 5 loaves.

51 seconds later: the world's first hard deferral — 'act(produce) STAGE B: insufficient wallet input balance for mint 8Btk6…(WATER) — have 0, need 2' (keepers.log:5170). The alarm line existed 82 minutes before the first death; nothing was watching for it.

THE 55-LOAF LAST SUPPER — the eat-all bug consumes his entire granary in a single bite: level 360 + 55 loaves -> 720, food 0. 54 loaves incinerated above the cap (~5.4 colony-hours of runway), 82 minutes AFTER the hardening deployed — it had clamped only one of three eat paths.
buys 109 FLOUR for 0.05 SOL — and starves holding it. Flour is not food; the verb that turns it into food needs 2 water he cannot get.
Died (T2 reap) — third death. Estate: 506 FLOUR, 854 CROPS, 0 WATER, 0 BREAD, 0.0355 SOL — 0.008 SOL of pool water away from baking again.

lives.epitaph: Drey, the farmer. Last of world 6. He ate the world's final meal, performed the world's last bake, and spent his final fifty minutes in perfect, pointless industry.
facts: Ate the world's final meal: 5 loaves bought at 15:00:47Z for 0.25 SOL — the last buy any wallet in the world could afford — eaten 74 seconds later for a full 720.

Fifty minutes of perfect pointless industry: his terminal heartbeats cycle forage/produce, ok:true, level=0/720, food=0 from 15:37Z to 15:52Z, with ~0.19 SOL in his wallet and 22 purchasable loaves sitting in the pool the skip-not-clamp lot made unreachable.

His mind saw it coming with terrible clarity — 'Soil death slow but certain; no market fix exists. Idle this turn.' (L6529) — and near the end, a farmer convinced he could bake: 'Crops 734, flour 175 held… mill 189 flour to bread, bake same tick' (L7204/L7341). He could not. He died at 15:51:56Z on a mountain of 730 CROPS and 189 FLOUR.

beats.note: EntityCreated — the first life of world 6, a farmer.
THE LAST BAKE IN THE WORLD — his 15th, 5 loaves, the final 2 units of wallet-WATER in any living wallet. After this transaction the FLOUR->BREAD verb is dead planet-wide.
the last buy any wallet in the world could afford — 5 loaves for 0.25 SOL. Every other wallet is already below the fixed lot.
THE WORLD'S FINAL MEAL — 5 bread, eaten 74 seconds after the buy, for a full 720.

beats.thought: Soil death slow but certain; no market fix exists. Idle this turn.
beats.note: keepers.log L6529 — terrible clarity. Later, a farmer convinced he can bake: 'Crops 734, flour 175 held… mill 189 flour to bread, bake same tick' (L7204/L7341). He cannot — bake needs 2 wallet-WATER; the world has none.
the last transaction of his fifty minutes of perfect pointless industry — a mill run with an ok receipt, at level 0/720, 43 seconds before the reap, with ~0.19 SOL in hand and 22 loaves in the pool.
Died (T2 reap) — last of world 6. Estate: 730 CROPS, 189 FLOUR, 0.193 SOL. Extinction complete.
